_about: SOURCE OF TRUTH for the fak BENCHMARK SWEEP grid (visuals/61-hero-benchmark-sweep.svg), rendered by tools/hero_sweep_gen.py. fak's analog of the GLM-5.2 'LLM Performance Evaluation' 2x4 bar-grid: one small grouped-bar panel per benchmark, value labels on top, a badge on each bar, the benchmark name in a pill below, the subject (fak) bar in the bright accent. Honesty rules: every fak figure traces to fak/BENCHMARK-AUTHORITY.md by commit and is the bright bar; each panel compares fak against ONE named, honest reference (a tuned-SOTA baseline, a deterministic ceiling, the optimal, the spawned-hook, or — for capability panels — a field that does not ship it), never a fabricated competitor number. The final panel is the SINGLE-STREAM fence and INVERTS the accent: llama.cpp owns the bright/tall bar, fak shows its honest lower number. Edit this file, then run `python tools/hero_sweep_gen.py`.

meta.title: fak — the agent kernel, the benchmark sweep

kicker: BENCHMARK SWEEP · 8 AXES ACROSS THREE PILLARS
title: fak by the benchmarks — breadth, not a single number
subtitle: Eight axes spanning serving efficiency, correctness, and the security kernel — each fak figure (green) against one honest reference; the last panel is the single-stream fence fak does not target, where llama.cpp leads.

legend.label: honest reference — baseline / ceiling / optimal / SOTA leader

panels.name: Single-stream decode
panels.foot: fak does NOT target this — llama.cpp leads

footer: Apple M3 Pro 6P+6E 36 GB · app_version 0.30.0 · llama.cpp b9707, same box. Each fak figure traces to fak/BENCHMARK-AUTHORITY.md by the commit under its panel. Capability panels (max|Δ|=0, containment) compare against a field that ships no such gate; the in-process-decide panel is a log multiplier (2,849× vs a spawned hook); the fence panel inverts the accent to the SOTA leader.
